title: Film and Video Editors
description: Edit moving images on film, video, or other media. May work with a producer or director to organize images for final production. May edit or synchronize soundtracks with images.

tasks.text: Organize and string together raw footage into a continuous whole according to scripts or the instructions of directors and producers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Edit films and videotapes to insert music, dialogue, and sound effects, to arrange films into sequences, and to correct errors, using editing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select and combine the most effective shots of each scene to form a logical and smoothly running story.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review footage sequence by sequence to become familiar with it before assembling it into a final product.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Set up and operate computer editing systems, electronic titling systems, video switching equipment, and digital video effects units to produce a final product.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Trim film segments to specified lengths and reassemble segments in sequences that present stories with maximum effect.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut shot sequences to different angles at specific points in scenes, making each individual cut as fluid and seamless as possible.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review assembled films or edited videotapes on screens or monitors to determine if corrections are necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Verify key numbers and time codes on materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manipulate plot, score, sound, and graphics to make the parts into a continuous whole, working closely with people in audio, visual, music, optical, or special effects departments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Program computerized graphic effects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study scripts to become familiar with production concepts and requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in film editing, assembling, and recording activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Determine the specific audio and visual effects and music necessary to complete films.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mark frames where a particular shot or piece of sound is to begin or end.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record needed sounds or obtain them from sound effects libraries.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Conduct film screenings for directors and members of production staffs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Discuss the sound requirements of pictures with sound effects editors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Piece sounds together to develop film soundtracks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with producers and directors concerning layout or editing approaches needed to increase dramatic or entertainment value of productions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop post-production models for films.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with music editors to select appropriate passages of music and develop production scores.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
